CP00-372 is a descriptor-only billing-core slice and every pack-level claim checks out:
- **Scope/distributions** consistent across baseline, manifest, plan, contract, and registry binding (40 units; deliverable and micro-title counts both sum to 40; CP00-371→CP00-372→CP00-373 wiring agrees).
- **No-write/no-real-data** enforced at row and result level; **production_ready correctly FALSE** with human approval gated for runtime opening.
- **Claude stays read-only** and non-final-approver; **Hermes is descriptor-only**, not product runtime.
- **Review-hardening readiness clean**: auth preflight passed, no invalid attempts, forbidden tools absent, post-review artifacts correctly absent.

One P3 informational note: a defensive conditional-guard block in the CP372 validator (`validators.js:1756`) targets row keys not present in this slice's vocabulary, so it never fires — but unconditional per-row guards plus result-level attestations fully cover the boundary, matching the already-closed CP365–CP371 packs. No P0/P1/P2 issues. Independent read-only inspection of all in-scope implementation (registry.js, service.js, validators.js, index.js, test/model.test.js, contracts/billing-core-contract.json, scripts/validate-rp12-billing-core-contract.mjs, README.md) plus pack evidence (manifest, command-evidence, baseline, readiness, validation-report, plan) confirms the pack-level claims hold. Scope and distributions are internally consistent across baseline/manifest/plan/contract/registry: deliverable counts (16/2/4/4/8/2/4) and micro-title counts (10/22/8) sum to 40; first/last unit, upstream CP00-371, and next CP00-373 wiring all agree. No-write/no-real-data boundaries are enforced at row level (descriptor_only/runtime_execution=false/customer_safe_errors_only/raw_payload_included=false) and result level (writes_product_state=false, no_real_data=true, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false, all dispatch flags false). The pack-level production_ready claim is correctly FALSE (implementation_and_plan_gates_passed_pending_final_closeout); enterprise trust is not claimed from local validation and human approval remains gated for runtime opening. Claude stays a read-only reviewer (tools Read/Grep/Glob, claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false). Hermes patterns are used only for descriptor-only evidence/receipt handling (receipt_shape ..._no_write; hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product=false), not product runtime. No P0/P1/P2 issues found.","findings":[{"id":"CP00-372-P3-01","severity":"P3","title":"Conditional no-leak guards in CP372 descriptor validator are inert for this slice's row vocabulary","file":"packages/billing/src/validators.js","line":1756,"details":"validateBillingCoreCp372P02TestHermesSliceDescriptor includes a defensive block that, per micro-phase, checks specific descriptor row keys (tenant_scope_field, state_transition_map, validation_helper, fixture_model, model_unit_test, invalid_reference_test, hermes_model_summary, claude_model_review_prompt) for their no-leak/no-write sub-flags. CP00-372 covers the M07 Test-And-Golden tail, M08 Hermes Evidence, and M09 Claude Review micro-phases, whose row keys are service/test/hermes vocabulary (e.g., tenant_boundary_precheck, state_transition_enforcement, validation_error_mapping, integration_smoke_case) and do not match any of those guarded keys, so each `if (rows.X && ...)` short-circuits and never fires. This is not a boundary gap: the unconditional per-row guards (descriptor_only=true, runtime_execution=false, customer_safe_errors_only=true, raw_payload_included=false) are asserted for every row, and freezeCp372Result stamps result-level attestations (writes_product_state=false, no_real_data=true, exposes_*=false, leak_detected=false, permission_bypass_detected=false, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false).
